reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL WASM run in headless Chrome (fresh profile → Download → ready): onnx-community/rtdetr_v2_r18vd-ONNX, transformers.js 3.7.5, q8. object-detection pipeline returned real boxes on sample-cats: cat 97.0%, remote 96.6%, cat 96.0%, sofa 87.7%, remote 53.9%. 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Class RTDetrV2 confirmed present in the 3.7.5 bundle (43 refs) so no version-pin needed. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w14dshots/rtdetr-v2-r18vd-desktop-light.png, -desktop-dark.png, -mobile-light.png
rubric.notes: Runnability verified BEFORE build via dynamic-import of the exact CDN build.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output is [{label, score, box:{xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax}}] in pixel coords (percentage:false), NMS-free (one box per object). See-inside table renders label/score/x/y/w/h per box; distinct-classes and total/shown counts correct. Semantically correct on the cats sample (2 cats + 2 remotes + sofa). Shape + semantics correct, not fake.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/rtdetr_v2_r18vd-ONNX, linked), task (object-detection), backend (WebAssembly), dtype (q8), ~21 MB, Apache-2.0. Inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js). conformance auto-checks (declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation/inference-off-main-thread) expected pass.

Agent verdict — fresh profile showed 'Download model (~21 MB)'; after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'ready' with a 'Clear cached model' control. Returning visit auto-inits from the transformers.js cache with no Load click (shared loader state machine: download-required → downloading → ready observed).

Agent verdict — streamed download progress via the shared loader; Retry on failure; #status live region (role=status aria-live) reports 'Detecting…' / counts / errors; webcam fallback panel for no-camera contexts. Runs on the WASM path (no GPU); headless no-GPU run produced real boxes. conformance live-status-region/errors-on-the-page expected pass; runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual=pass.

Agent verdict — dropzone (keyboard-operable), 5 sample thumbs (Enter/Space), Detect button, Start/Stop webcam, score-threshold range (filters cached boxes with no re-run), and the 'Compare v2 vs v1' button all operable. Ladder pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model) each load with 8–11 controls, 0 console errors, 0 overflow (headless matrix).
Agent verdict — boxes, counts, the see-inside table and the v2-vs-v1 head-to-head are all produced by on-device runs (real output sample recorded above). No canned result anywhere.

Agent verdict — every img has alt; every button has an accessible name; canvas has aria-label; range slider has a <label> + <output>; live regions announce state. conformance images-have-alt/buttons-have-names expected pass.

Agent verdict — desktop 1280 (light+dark) and mobile 360×740 DPR3 render with no horizontal overflow (measured 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages, both classes), legible text, wrapping at-a-glance table, canvas scales to width, control rows flex-wrap. screenshots reviewed (w14dshots/rtdetr-v2-r18vd-*). conformance responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile expected pass.

Agent verdict — all detection runs in a Web Worker; the threshold slider filters a cached box list (no re-run); the webcam loop schedules the next frame only after the last finishes (no pile-up). Main thread stays responsive; 0 console errors during interaction. guidance break-up-long-tasks + identify-inp-causes applied.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread work; yield to keep the UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference is off the main thread in worker.js; the webcam loop is self-pacing (schedules the next frame only after the previous detect resolves), so it never blocks or piles up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js runs the object-detection pipeline; index.html loop() gate (busy flag + setTimeout) verified; 0 console errors during detect

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep INP low — keep heavy JS off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dragging the score slider only re-filters and re-draws a cached array (no model call); detection and the webcam loop run in the worker.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: thresh 'input' handler calls render() over cached allDetections; no re-inference on slide

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel canvas overlay without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; wrap control rows; canvas scales to container; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — control rows use flex-wrap; the stage canvas is inline-size:100% (shared DETR_CSS); at-a-glance table wraps. Measured 0 px overflow at 360px on index + all ladder pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless overflow=0 desktop+mobile; mobile screenshot reviewed

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; keyboard-operable custom controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #status; dropzone/canvas/sample-thumbs are keyboard-operable with aria-labels; slider has label+output.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names/images-have-alt derived; keyboard handlers on dropzone + thumbs

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use modern flow/grid layout; prevent overflow via min-inline-size:0 on flex children.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-up/stage layouts use flex with min-inline-size:0 so wide canvases and tables never force horizontal scroll.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: multi-model + practical stage columns; overflow=0 verified

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css.

openQuestions: How much does the v2 flexible decoder actually shift boxes vs v1 on typical browser images at q8? The head-to-head surfaces per-image deltas but a systematic sweep would quantify it.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for rtdetr-v2-r18vd (honest WASM fallback retained) to raise webcam FPS on capable devices, as an additive enhancement.
Add the larger rtdetr-v2-r50vd variant as a separate demo so the accuracy/latency trade within v2 is visible (pending catalogue entry already exists).

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for rtdetr-v2-r18vd (RT-DETRv2, the improved real-time DETR — flexible/discrete-sampling decoder). Runnability verified before build (RTDetrV2 class in transformers.js 3.7.5, no pin). Real download + on-device run verified in headless Chrome (cat 97%/remote 96.6%/sofa 87.7% on the cats sample), light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (0 overflow, legible, operable controls), console + network clean. All 4 ladder pages load clean. Distinct from the existing RT-DETR(v1) page: same size/backbone, so the signature contrast is a v2-vs-v1 accuracy/cleanliness head-to-head, not v1's speed-vs-heavy-DETR race.
